text: The NFT market is experiencing a utility-driven comeback in 2026, with the Bored Ape Yacht Club (BAYC) serving as a key cultural and economic anchor. BAYC's legacy as a pioneering NFT collection that combined digital art with community membership and IP rights continues to influence the space. The project has evolved with new developments like ApeChain (a Layer-3 blockchain) and ApeCoin, supporting scalable transactions and governance. Nostalgia for BAYC's early innovation fuels renewed interest and stability in the NFT sector, which is shifting focus from speculative hype to real-world applications in gaming, digital identity, and tokenized assets. This renaissance is expected to drive economic empowerment, cultural richness, and technological advancement in the broader Web3 ecosystem.

text: Research on Hyperliquid's user profitability reveals that approximately 29% of native frontend users are profitable trading perpetuals, a rate comparable to traditional CFD brokers. Builder apps integrated with Hyperliquid show slightly lower profitability, likely due to user selection and builder fees. Advanced trading terminals and tools tend to have higher profitability among their users, indicating that product design and user education impact outcomes. The study highlights that while builder fees affect profitability marginally, the main factor is the type of users attracted by different frontends. Retention and user profitability are closely linked, suggesting builders should focus on helping users trade better to improve business outcomes. This research provides valuable insights for developers and traders in the decentralized perpetuals ecosystem.
author: elfa_2046965547045113958

text: Prediction market platforms Polymarket and Kalshi have announced plans to launch perpetual futures (perps) trading products, expanding their offerings beyond traditional prediction markets. This move aims to capture the large derivatives market and provide users with high-frequency, leveraged trading options. Kalshi, a CFTC-regulated designated contract market, and Polymarket US are positioned to offer compliant perpetual futures to U.S. retail and institutional investors. These developments are seen as a strategic response to competition from decentralized perpetuals platforms like Hyperliquid, which is integrating prediction markets into its platform. The convergence of prediction markets and perpetual futures reflects a broader industry trend toward comprehensive multi-asset trading platforms. Market participants view the entrance of Polymarket and Kalshi into perps as bullish for the overall category, indirectly benefiting Hyperliquid by normalizing perpetuals in the regulatory landscape and accelerating adoption.
author: elfa_2046900521869643962

text: Hyperliquid ($HYPE) has demonstrated significant growth and innovation in the decentralized perpetuals market, burning 42.9 million tokens ($1.55 billion) and routing 99% of protocol revenue into buybacks. The platform's open interest surged 815% in four months, with HIP-3 tradfi perpetuals increasing from 3% to 47% of platform volume since October 2025. Notably, oil perpetuals hit $7.3 billion volume in a single day during the Iran crisis when CME was closed. Coinbase and Bybit formed a partnership specifically to compete against Hyperliquid, highlighting its market impact despite having only 11 employees. Hyperliquid is also expanding its ecosystem with new features like prediction markets (HIP-4) and has a growing number of builders creating on top of it, enhancing its composability and user experience. The platform's native token $HYPE is viewed as undervalued by some traders, with bullish long-term sentiment. Additionally, Hyperliquid has integrated perpetual futures into non-custodial DeFi wallets, enabling leveraged positions with self-custodied BTC as collateral.

Risk sentiment has stabilised, but the macro overhang remains firmly in place. Oil near $100 signals persistence in supply disruption, keeping inflation sticky while weighing on growth. This leaves markets trapped between elevated price pressures and a softening demand outlook, complicating the policy path. For crypto, BTC's rebound is driven more by reduced tail risk than improved fundamentals. Open interest has rebuilt while funding remains negative, indicating fresh shorts entering rather than capitulating. This keeps squeeze dynamics in play, but conviction remains shallow. Options markets echo the same tone. Front-end vols around 40 vol remain subdued relative to realised, skew still favours downside protection, and term structure is only modestly upward sloping. Positioning continues to point to range-bound conditions rather than a sustained breakout. The path forward remains anchored to oil and policy. text: Four Horsemen of the AIpocalypse AI industry is in a hysteria-driven bubble built on unsustainable economics, 4 warning signs: 1. Anthropic can't keep the lights on: Claude's services run at 99% uptime (far below the industry-standard 99.99%), and the new Opus 4.7 model reportedly performs *worse* than 4.6 while burning more tokens. Users report hallucinations, laziness, and regressions 2. Compute demand is artificially inflated: Of 114GW of AI data center capacity supposedly coming by 2028, only 15.2GW is actually under construction 3. NVIDIA's numbers don't add up: NVIDIA claims $1 trillion in sales visibility through 2027, but Zitron calculates that existing data center construction only supports about $285 billion in GPU revenue. He suggests NVIDIA is selling years of GPUs in advance with nowhere to put them, pointing to ballooning inventory at Taiwanese ODMs like Quanta (up from $10.5B to $16.3B in one quarter) as early evidence 4. The subsidy era is ending: Companies are spending up to 10% of engineering headcount costs on AI tokens, with Goldman Sachs warning it could hit 100% within quarters. Uber blew through its entire 2026 AI budget in a few months. Microsoft is moving GitHub Copilot to token-based billing and pausing new paid signups; Anthropic has already shifted enterprise customers to API rates. Once users face real costs, Zitron thinks demand collapses

text: Threadguy - An Unfiltered Conversation with Cobie Sees Coinbase as one of the highest-leverage ways to impact the industry. K-Shaped thesis: crypto is succeeding at many things it promised - stablecoins (Tempo, DoorDash paying drivers), prediction markets (Polymarket/Kalshi duopoly), Hyperliquid, Trade XYZ - but none of that upside accrues to liquid crypto assets investors can actually buy. Success is being privatized Biggest worry for DeFi is Anthropic-style models turning protocols into "financial bounties" for attacker Wasn't worried about Saylor/MSTR until his 70-year-old dentist told him 80% of his portfolio was in MSTR. That's when he realized it had broken containment. Sees the stretch strategy as shifting from tailwind to overhang On young talent leaving crypto for AI: every onboarding wave (2013 alts, 2017 ICOs, 2021 DeFi/NFTs, memecoins) had one thing in common - normal people made money fast. AI feels like that now. Doesn't know if/when crypto gets another wave, but history repeats SpaceX, Anthropic, OpenAI went 0 → multi-trillion with zero upside for normal people. Calls it a "betrayal of the capitalist system" and the kind of wealth inequality that historically leads to social revolution. Interesting crypto bull case: airdrops as capitalism's answer to UBI- Hyperliquid is the flagship proof this model works
